name: markdown2

metadata.name: markdown2
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: A fast and complete Python implementation of Markdown

documentation:
markdown2: A fast and complete Python implementation of Markdown.

Markdown is a text-to-HTML filter; it translates an easy-to-read /
easy-to-write structured text format into HTML.  Markdown's text
format is most similar to that of plain text email, and supports
features such as headers, *emphasis*, code blocks, blockquotes, and
links.  -- http://daringfireball.net/projects/markdown/

This is a fast and complete Python implementation of the Markdown
spec. See http://github.com/trentm/python-markdown2 for more info.

modules.name: markdown2
documentation:
A fast and complete Python implementation of Markdown. [from http://daringfireball.net/projects/markdown/] > Markdown is a text-to-HTML filter; it translates an easy-to-read / > easy-to-write structured text format into HTML. Markdown's text > format is most similar to that of plain text email, and supports > features such as headers, *emphasis*, code blocks, blockquotes, and > links. > > Markdown's syntax is designed not as a generic markup language, but > specifically to serve as a front-end to (X)HTML. You can use span-level > HTML tags anywhere in a Markdown document, and you can use block level > HTML tags (like
and as well). Module usage: >>> import markdown2 >>> markdown2.markdown("*boo!*") # or use `html = markdown_path(PATH)` u'

boo!

\n' >>> markdowner = Markdown() >>> markdowner.convert("*boo!*") u'

boo!

\n' >>> markdowner.convert("**boom!**") u'

boom!

\n' This implementation of Markdown implements the full "core" syntax plus a number of extras (e.g., code syntax coloring, footnotes) as described on .
!/usr/bin/env python
Copyright (c) 2012 Trent Mick.
Copyright (c) 2007-2008 ActiveState Corp.
License: MIT (http://www.opensource.org/licenses/mit-license.php)

functions.name: markdown2.calculate_toc_html

Return the HTML for the current TOC.

This expects the `_toc` attribute to have been set on this instance.
---- internal support functions

functions.name: markdown2._slugify
documentation:
Normalizes string, converts to lowercase, removes non-alpha characters,
and converts spaces to hyphens.

From Django's "django/template/defaultfilters.py".

functions.name: markdown2._curry
documentation: # end of http://code.activestate.com/recipes/577257/ }}}
From http://aspn.activestate.com/ASPN/Cookbook/Python/Recipe/52549

functions.name: markdown2._regex_from_encoded_pattern
documentation:
'foo'    -> re.compile(re.escape('foo'))
'/foo/'  -> re.compile('foo')
'/foo/i' -> re.compile('foo', re.I)
Recipe: regex_from_encoded_pattern (1.0)

functions.name: markdown2._dedentlines
documentation:
_dedentlines(lines, tabsize=8, skip_first_line=False) -> dedented lines

    "lines" is a list of lines to dedent.
    "tabsize" is the tab width to use for indent width calculations.
    "skip_first_line" is a boolean indicating if the first line should
        be skipped for calculating the indent width and for dedenting.
        This is sometimes useful for docstrings and similar.

Same as dedent() except operates on a sequence of lines. Note: the
lines list is modified **in-place**.
Recipe: dedent (0.1.2)

functions.name: markdown2._dedent
documentation:
_dedent(text, tabsize=8, skip_first_line=False) -> dedented text

    "text" is the text to dedent.
    "tabsize" is the tab width to use for indent width calculations.
    "skip_first_line" is a boolean indicating if the first line should
        be skipped for calculating the indent width and for dedenting.
        This is sometimes useful for docstrings and similar.

textwrap.dedent(s), but don't expand tabs to spaces

functions.name: markdown2._xml_oneliner_re_from_tab_width
documentation: Standalone XML processing instruction regex.

functions.name: markdown2._xml_escape_attr
documentation:
Escape the given string for use in an HTML/XML tag attribute.

By default this doesn't bother with escaping `'` to `'`, presuming that
the tag attribute is surrounded by double quotes.

functions.name: markdown2._html_escape_url
documentation:
Replace special characters that are potentially malicious in url string.

Args:
    charset: don't escape characters from this charset. Currently the only
        exception is for '+' when charset=='base64'

documentation: ---- exceptions

class_variables.name: markdown2.Stage.LINKS
documentation: and auto links

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown.convert
documentation: Convert the given text.

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown.postprocess
documentation:
A hook for subclasses to do some postprocessing of the html, if
desired. This is called before unescaping of special chars and
unhashing of raw HTML spans.

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown.preprocess
documentation:
A hook for subclasses to do some preprocessing of the Markdown, if
desired. This is called after basic formatting of the text, but prior
to any extras, safe mode, etc. processing.

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown._get_emacs_vars
documentation:
Return a dictionary of emacs-style local variables.

Parsing is done loosely according to this spec (and according to
some in-practice deviations from this):
http://www.gnu.org/software/emacs/manual/html_node/emacs/Specifying-File-Variables.html#Specifying-File-Variables

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown._detab_line
documentation:
Recusively convert tabs to spaces in a single line.

Called from _detab().

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown._detab
documentation:
Iterate text line by line and convert tabs to spaces.

```pycon
>>> m = Markdown()
>>> m._detab("\tfoo")
'    foo'
>>> m._detab("  \tfoo")
'    foo'
>>> m._detab("\t  foo")
'      foo'
>>> m._detab("  foo")
'  foo'
>>> m._detab("  foo\n\tbar\tblam")
'  foo\n    bar blam'
```

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown._hash_html_blocks
documentation:
Hashify HTML blocks We only want to do this for block-level HTML tags, such as headers, lists, and tables. That's because we still want to wrap

s around "paragraphs" that are wrapped in non-block-level tags, such as anchors, phrase emphasis, and spans. The list of tags we're looking for is hard-coded. @param raw {boolean} indicates if these are raw HTML blocks in the original source. It makes a difference in "safe" mode.

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown._strict_tag_block_sub
documentation:
Finds and substitutes HTML blocks within blocks of text

Args:
    text: the text to search
    html_tags_re: a regex pattern of HTML block tags to match against.
        For example, `Markdown._block_tags_a`
    callback: callback function that receives the found HTML text block and returns a new str
    allow_indent: allow matching HTML blocks that are not completely outdented

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown._strip_footnote_definitions
documentation:
A footnote definition looks like this:

    [^note-id]: Text of the note.

        May include one or more indented paragraphs.

Where,
- The 'note-id' can be pretty much anything, though typically it
  is the number of the footnote.
- The first paragraph may start on the next line, like so:

    [^note-id]:
        Text of the note.

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown._unhash_html_spans
documentation:
Recursively unhash a block of text

Args:
    spans: unhash anything from `self.html_spans`
    code: unhash code blocks

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown._find_non_whitespace
documentation:
Returns the index of the first non-whitespace character in text
after (and including) start

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown._find_balanced
documentation:
Returns the index where the open_c and close_c characters balance
out - the same number of open_c and close_c are encountered - or the
end of string if it's reached before the balance point is found.

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown._extract_url_and_title
documentation: Extracts the url and (optional) title from the tail of a link

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown._protect_url
documentation:
Function that passes a URL through `_html_escape_url` to remove any nasty characters,
and then hashes the now "safe" URL to prevent other safety mechanisms from tampering
with it (eg: escaping "&" in URL parameters)

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown._safe_href
documentation:
_safe_href is adapted from pagedown's Markdown.Sanitizer.js
From: https://github.com/StackExchange/pagedown/blob/master/LICENSE.txt
Original Showdown code copyright (c) 2007 John Fraser
Modifications and bugfixes (c) 2009 Dana Robinson
Modifications and bugfixes (c) 2009-2014 Stack Exchange Inc.

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown._do_links
documentation: Turn Markdown link shortcuts into XHTML and tags. This is a combination of Markdown.pl's _DoAnchors() and _DoImages(). They are done together because that simplified the approach. It was necessary to use a different approach than Markdown.pl because of the lack of atomic matching support in Python's regex engine used in $g_nested_brackets.

documentation: Handles processing markdown headers

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown._h_tag_sub

TODO: this function is only referenced by the `FencedCodeBlocks`

Args:

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown._html_class_str_from_tag
documentation:
Get the appropriate ' class="..."' string (note the leading
space), if any, for the given tag.

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown._do_code_blocks
documentation: Process Markdown `<pre><code>` blocks.

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown._encode_code
documentation:
Encode/escape certain characters inside Markdown code runs.
The point is that in code, these characters are literals,
and lose their special Markdown meanings.

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown._hash_span
documentation:
Wrapper around `_hash_text` that also adds the hash to `self.hash_spans`,
meaning it will be automatically unhashed during conversion.

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown._uniform_outdent
documentation:
Removes the smallest common leading indentation from each (non empty)
line of `text` and returns said indent along with the outdented text.

Args:
    min_outdent: make sure the smallest common whitespace is at least this size
    max_outdent: the maximum amount a line can be outdented by

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown._uniform_indent
documentation:
Uniformly indent a block of text by a fixed amount

Args:
    text: the text to indent
    indent: a string containing the indent to apply
    include_empty_lines: don't remove whitespace only lines
    indent_empty_lines: indent whitespace only lines with the rest of the text

methods.name: markdown2.Markdown._match_overlaps_substr
documentation: Checks if a regex match overlaps with a substring in the given text.

class_variables.name: markdown2.Markdown.html_removed_text
documentation: placeholder removed text that does not trigger bold

class_variables.name: markdown2.Markdown.html_removed_text_compat

class_variables.name: markdown2.Markdown.stage
documentation: Current "stage" of markdown conversion taking place

class_variables.name: markdown2.Markdown.order
documentation:
Same as `Stage` but will be +/- 0.5 of the value of `Stage`.
This allows extras to check if they are running before or after a particular stage
with `if md.order < md.stage`.

class_variables.name: markdown2.Markdown._key_val_dict_pat
documentation: grp0: key, grp1: value, grp2: multiline value

classes.name: markdown2.MarkdownWithExtras

class_variables.name: markdown2.MarkdownWithExtras.extras
documentation: type: ignore

A markdowner class that enables most extras:

- footnotes
- fenced-code-blocks (only highlights code if 'pygments' Python module on path)

These are not included:
- pyshell (specific to Python-related documenting)
- code-friendly (because it *disables* part of the syntax)
- link-patterns (because you need to specify some actual
  link-patterns anyway)

classes.name: markdown2.Extra

methods.name: markdown2.Extra.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    md: An instance of `Markdown`
    options: a dict of settings to alter the extra's behaviour

methods.name: markdown2.Extra.deregister
documentation: Removes the class from the extras registry and unsets its execution order.

methods.name: markdown2.Extra.register
documentation:
Registers the class for use with `Markdown` and calculates its execution order based on
the `order` class attribute.

methods.name: markdown2.Extra.run
documentation:
Run the extra against the given text.

Returns:
    The new text after being modified by the extra

methods.name: markdown2.Extra.test
documentation:
Check a section of markdown to see if this extra should be run upon it.
The default implementation will always return True but it's recommended to override
this behaviour to improve performance.

class_variables.name: markdown2.Extra.name
documentation:
An identifiable name that users can use to invoke the extra
in the Markdown class

class_variables.name: markdown2.Extra.order
documentation:
Tuple of two iterables containing the stages/extras this extra will run before and
after, respectively
Extras
Base classes

classes.name: markdown2.ItalicAndBoldProcessor
documentation:
An ABC that provides hooks for dealing with italics and bold syntax.
This class is set to trigger both before AND after the italics and bold stage.
This allows any child classes to intercept instances of bold or italic syntax and
change the output or hash it to prevent it from being processed.

After the I&B stage any hashes in the `hash_tables` instance variable are replaced.

classes.name: markdown2.Admonitions
documentation: Enable parsing of RST admonitions
User facing extras

classes.name: markdown2.Alerts
documentation:
Markdown Alerts as per
https://docs.github.com/en/get-started/writing-on-github/getting-started-with-writing-and-formatting-on-github/basic-writing-and-formatting-syntax#alerts

Replace single new line characters with

Options for the `Breaks` extra

classes.name: markdown2.CodeFriendly
documentation: Disable _ and __ for em and strong.

classes.name: markdown2.FencedCodeBlocks

methods.name: markdown2.FencedCodeBlocks._code_block_with_lexer_sub
documentation:
Args:
    codeblock: the codeblock to format
    leading_indent: the indentation to prefix the block with
    lexer (pygments.Lexer): the lexer to use

methods.name: markdown2.FencedCodeBlocks.tags
documentation:
Returns the tags that the encoded code block will be wrapped in, based
upon the lexer name.

This function can be overridden by subclasses to piggy-back off of the
fenced code blocks syntax (see `Mermaid` extra).

Returns:
    The opening and closing tags, as strings within a tuple

Allows a code block to not have to be indented
by fencing it with '```' on a line before and after. Based on
<http://github.github.com/github-flavored-markdown/> with support for
syntax highlighting.

classes.name: markdown2.Latex

class_variables.name: markdown2.Latex._pre_code_block_re
documentation: Wraped in <pre>

class_variables.name: markdown2.Latex._triple_re
documentation: Wrapped in a code block ```

class_variables.name: markdown2.Latex._single_re
documentation: Wrapped in a single `
Convert $ and $$ to <math> and </math> tags for inline and block math.

classes.name: markdown2.LinkPatterns
documentation:
Auto-link given regex patterns in text (e.g. bug number
references, revision number references).

classes.name: markdown2.MarkdownInHTML
documentation:
Allow the use of `markdown="1"` in a block HTML tag to
have markdown processing be done on its contents. Similar to
<http://michelf.com/projects/php-markdown/extra/#markdown-attr> but with
some limitations.

classes.name: markdown2.MiddleWordEm

methods.name: markdown2.MiddleWordEm.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    md: the markdown instance
    options: can be bool for backwards compatibility but will be converted to a dict
        in the constructor. All options are:
        - allowed (bool): whether to allow emphasis in the middle of a word.
            If `options` is a bool it will be placed under this key.
Allows or disallows emphasis syntax in the middle of words, defaulting to allow. Disabling this means that `this_text_here` will not be converted to `thistexthere`.

classes.name: markdown2.Numbering
documentation:
Support of generic counters.  Non standard extension to
allow sequential numbering of figures, tables, equations, exhibits etc.

classes.name: markdown2.PyShell
documentation:
Treats unindented Python interactive shell sessions as <code>
blocks.

classes.name: markdown2.SmartyPants

methods.name: markdown2.SmartyPants.run
documentation:
Fancifies 'single quotes', "double quotes", and apostrophes.
Converts --, ---, and ... into en dashes, em dashes, and ellipses.

Inspiration is: <http://daringfireball.net/projects/smartypants/>
See "test/tm-cases/smarty_pants.text" for a full discussion of the
support here and
<http://code.google.com/p/python-markdown2/issues/detail?id=42> for a
discussion of some diversion from the original SmartyPants.

Replaces ' and " with curly quotation marks or curly
apostrophes.  Replaces --, ---, ..., and . . . with en dashes, em dashes,
and ellipses.

classes.name: markdown2.Strike
documentation: Text inside of double tilde is ~~strikethrough~~

classes.name: markdown2.Tables

methods.name: markdown2.Tables.run
documentation:
Copying PHP-Markdown and GFM table syntax. Some regex borrowed from
https://github.com/michelf/php-markdown/blob/lib/Michelf/Markdown.php#L2538

Tables using the same format as GFM
<https://help.github.com/articles/github-flavored-markdown#tables> and
PHP-Markdown Extra <https://michelf.ca/projects/php-markdown/extra/#table>.

classes.name: markdown2.Underline
documentation: Text inside of double dash is --underlined--.

classes.name: markdown2._WavedromExtraOpts

documentation:
Use the `wavedrom` library to convert diagrams to SVGs and embed them directly.
This will only work if the `wavedrom` library has been installed.

Defaults to `True`
Options for the `Wavedrom` extra

classes.name: markdown2.Wavedrom
documentation: Support for generating Wavedrom digital timing diagrams

classes.name: markdown2.WikiTables
documentation:
Google Code Wiki-style tables. See
<http://code.google.com/p/support/wiki/WikiSyntax#Tables>.

classes.name: markdown2.UnicodeWithAttrs
documentation:
A subclass of unicode used for the return value of conversion to
possibly attach some attributes. E.g. the "toc_html" attribute when
the "toc" extra is used.

classes.name: markdown2._memoized

methods.name: markdown2._memoized.__repr__
documentation: Return the function's docstring.

Decorator that caches a function's return value each time it is called.
If called later with the same arguments, the cached value is returned, and
not re-evaluated.

http://wiki.python.org/moin/PythonDecoratorLibrary

classes.name: markdown2._NoReflowFormatter
documentation: An argparse formatter that does NOT reflow the description.
